curriculum.summary: Use the tutorial compass to choose a path, inspect prerequisites, and understand what mastery means at each level.
curriculum.title: Hermes Tutorial Compass

curriculum.summary: Get one plain Hermes chat working first, then add tools, profiles, gateways, and automations gradually.
curriculum.title: Hermes Setup Coach

curriculum.summary: Learn core Hermes words before following procedures that depend on them.
curriculum.title: Hermes Concept Glossary

curriculum.summary: Practice safe commands such as `hermes --help`, `/help`, `/status`, and read-only inspection before changing settings.
curriculum.title: Hermes Command Lab

curriculum.summary: Ask Hermes to slow down, define jargon, and summarize each step when learning a new concept.
curriculum.title: Hermes Accessibility Coach

curriculum.summary: Tools are capabilities, not toys: use the smallest safe toolset that proves the task.
curriculum.title: Hermes Tools Lab

curriculum.summary: Use `hermes config` for non-secret settings and setup/auth flows for secrets.
curriculum.title: Hermes Config Lab

curriculum.summary: Profiles isolate Hermes state, not the operating-system user or filesystem; sandboxing is a separate terminal/backend choice.
curriculum.title: Hermes Safety Sandbox Lab

curriculum.summary: Start from a safe recipe, customize only the placeholders, then validate with live Hermes output.
curriculum.title: Hermes Example Gallery

curriculum.summary: Use skills for reusable procedures and checklists; use plugins only when Hermes needs new capabilities.
curriculum.title: Hermes Skills Lab

curriculum.summary: Save durable facts in memory, retrieve past task context with session search, and keep procedures in skills.
curriculum.title: Hermes Memory Lab

curriculum.summary: A profile has its own Hermes home; its working directory and system sandbox are separate choices.
curriculum.title: Hermes Profiles Lab

curriculum.summary: Cron jobs run in fresh sessions with self-contained prompts; TUI-local jobs do not live-deliver back into the TUI.
curriculum.title: Hermes Cron Automation Lab

curriculum.summary: A bot with terminal tools needs strict authorization, pairing, and allowlists before it should be trusted.
curriculum.title: Hermes Gateway Lab

curriculum.summary: Prefer MCP for external tool ecosystems with existing servers; prefer plugins for Hermes-native capabilities.
curriculum.title: Hermes Mcp Lab

curriculum.summary: Start with `hermes doctor`, `hermes status`, targeted logs, and the exact command that failed.
curriculum.title: Hermes Troubleshooting Lab

curriculum.summary: Good Hermes prompts define objective, context, write scope, source-of-truth checks, and validation threshold.
curriculum.title: Hermes Prompting Lab

curriculum.summary: Map each goal to profile primitives: identity, model, configuration, terminal, tools, skills, memory, automation, safety, performance, validation, and portability.
curriculum.title: Hermes Profile Builder Lab

curriculum.summary: Choose the lightest correct extension surface before writing plugin code; validate manifest, register APIs, handler JSON contracts, and load behavior.
curriculum.title: Hermes Plugin Builder Lab

curriculum.summary: Tutorial content is helpful guidance, but official Hermes docs and live CLI output win when they differ.
curriculum.title: Hermes Docs Sync Tutor

curriculum.summary: Assessment should test recall, practical execution, safety judgment, troubleshooting judgment, and design competence.
curriculum.title: Hermes Assessment Certifier

curriculum.summary: Capstones prove that a learner can combine Hermes primitives safely, explain tradeoffs, and validate results.
curriculum.title: Hermes Capstone Orchestrator

curriculum.summary: Track local tutorial progress under the active user profile only.
curriculum.title: Learning Progress

description: Central learning-path navigator for Hermes Agent tutorial profiles.

examples.summary: Start with read-only help, docs, or validation output before writing files or changing configuration.
examples.title: Safe first step for Hermes Tutorial Compass

glossary.SOUL.md: Profile-level identity and voice instructions; not project rules or secrets.
glossary.approval prompt: A safety prompt before Hermes runs potentially risky shell actions.
glossary.context file: A project instruction file such as AGENTS.md or .hermes.md loaded from the working directory.
glossary.cron: Hermes scheduled jobs: one-shot or recurring agent/script runs with explicit delivery targets.
glossary.gateway: The messaging bridge that lets Hermes operate through platforms such as Telegram, Discord, Slack, or email.
glossary.mcp: Model Context Protocol: a standard way to connect Hermes to external tool servers.
glossary.memory: Compact durable facts that survive sessions; not a task diary or document store.
glossary.model: The specific LLM chosen from a provider.
glossary.plugin: A Hermes extension package that can register tools, commands, hooks, bundled skills, provider backends, or data files.
glossary.profile: A separate Hermes home with its own config, skills, memory, sessions, cron, plugins, and gateway state. It is not an OS sandbox.
glossary.provider: The service or backend that supplies model inference, such as OpenRouter, Nous, OpenAI, or a custom endpoint.
glossary.sandbox: An execution boundary such as Docker or remote backend; profiles alone are not sandboxes.
glossary.session: A conversation history with its own context, title, and state.
glossary.skill: A reusable procedure or reference document Hermes can load when a task calls for it.
glossary.terminal backend: Where terminal commands run: local machine, Docker, SSH, Modal, or another configured backend.
glossary.tool: A capability the model can call, such as reading a file, searching the web, or running a terminal command.
glossary.toolset: A grouped set of tools that can be enabled or disabled together to control capability and prompt size.

lesson: Use the tutorial compass to choose a path, inspect prerequisites, and understand what mastery means at each level.

lessons.body: Use the tutorial compass to choose a path, inspect prerequisites, and understand what mastery means at each level.
lessons.summary: Use the tutorial compass to choose a path, inspect prerequisites, and understand what mastery means at each level.
lessons.title: Hermes Tutorial Compass

name: hermes-tutorial-compass

quiz.question: Why is a Hermes profile not the same as a sandbox?
When should tutorial content be a skill instead of plugin code?
What must you do before publishing a public profile package?

safety_note: Use official Hermes docs and live CLI output; never ask learners to paste secrets into chat.
